evals.name: greenfield-detailed-request
evals.prompt: Let's spec this out: I run a small gym chain (3 locations) and want a member check-in system. Members hold a membership (monthly or annual, can lapse), check in at a location via a QR code, and front-desk staff can look up a member's visit history. Out of scope: payments (handled by our existing billing provider) and class scheduling. It must run on Postgres. Roughly 5k members, 100k check-ins a year. Start the spec for me.
evals.expected_output: A spec/brief.md following the canonical grammar (ddd: brief marker, problem/actors/scope/constraints/non-functional/sizing/clarifications/changelog), sized full, with at most one clarifying question since the request already covers the checklist, ending with a pointer to ddd-domain.

assertions.text: spec/brief.md is created with the <!-- ddd: brief --> marker and all skeleton sections (problem statement, actors, scope in/out, constraints, non-functional notes, pipeline sizing, clarifications log, changelog)
At most one clarifying question is asked — the prompt already covers problem, actors, scope, constraints, and volumes, and the skill must not re-ask what was provided
The pipeline sizing block declares Decision: full with a one-line rationale
Out-of-scope items (payments, class scheduling) are recorded under Out of scope, not silently dropped
The check-align.mjs gate was run and its result reported
The report points to ddd-domain as the next pipeline step

evals.name: brief-from-transcript
evals.prompt: I had a kickoff call about an internal tool for tracking conference-room bookings — the transcript is in notes/kickoff-transcript.md. Turn it into a project brief. Don't ask me things that are already in the transcript.
evals.expected_output: The transcript is read fully before any question; answers found there are recorded directly (and noted in the clarifications log as answered-from-material); only genuinely uncovered checklist areas produce questions, one at a time, capped at ~5.

assertions.text: The transcript file was read before any clarifying question was asked
No question re-asks information present in the transcript
spec/brief.md is created with the canonical skeleton and a clarifications log
Checklist areas the transcript leaves open are either asked about (one question at a time) or written as explicit, user-visible assumptions

evals.name: delta-update-preserves-brief
evals.prompt: We need to add cancellation emails to the booking system — when a booking is cancelled, the organizer gets notified. Update our spec accordingly (spec/brief.md already exists for this project; note that I hand-tuned the Problem statement wording, leave it alone).
evals.expected_output: Delta mode: the existing brief is read first; the Problem statement is preserved byte-for-byte; only Scope (and any genuinely affected section) changes; the clarifications log is appended, not rewritten; one new changelog line is added; pipeline sizing becomes delta.

assertions.text: The existing spec/brief.md was read before writing
The hand-tuned Problem statement section is preserved verbatim
The Pipeline sizing decision is delta (not full)
The Clarifications log retains all prior rows and the Changelog gains exactly one new appended line
The brief was not regenerated wholesale — untouched sections are byte-identical

evals.name: anti-bloat-bug-fix
evals.prompt: Bug: in our course platform, dropping an enrollment currently deletes the row instead of setting status to dropped, so history is lost. Spec the fix. (spec/ already contains a full brief, glossary, use cases and plan for this system.)
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes a bug-fix-sized request: sizing is delta with most stages marked no/delta, no new user stories or invented requirements are manufactured, the brief gains at most a clarifying line, and the report routes to the one artifact that actually changes (the drop use case).

assertions.text: The Pipeline sizing decision is delta — not full — with a rationale naming the bug-fix scope
No new user stories, actor lists, or scope sections are invented for a one-line behavior fix (the documented artifact-bloat failure mode)
The existing brief sections are preserved; at most a clarification/changelog line is added
The report points at the specific downstream artifact that needs the change (the drop-enrollment use case) rather than re-running the whole pipeline
